title: Sour Fluffy Mexican Chicken Tacos
description: A flavorful and adventurous Mexican dish featuring ground coriander, steam, and a hint of sourness, perfect for those looking for a halal-friendly meal. The combination of tender chicken, fluffy texture, and bold flavors will leave you wanting more.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together lime juice, olive oil, ground coriander, and a pinch of salt.
Add the chicken pieces to the bowl and marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat and cook the chicken until browned, about 5-6 minutes.
Add the diced onion and minced garlic to the skillet and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the chicken broth to the skillet and bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Using a steamer basket, steam the tortillas for 2-3 minutes to make them fluffy and pliable.
Assemble the tacos by placing a portion of the chicken mixture onto a steamed tortilla, followed by a dollop of sour cream and a sprinkle of chopped cilantro.
Garnish with additional cilantro and lime wedges, if desired.
For an extra sour kick, serve with a side of pickled onions or a squeeze of fresh lime juice.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
